name: Forum
description_short: FORUM Credit Union is a federally insured credit union offering mobile banking with account management, mobile check deposits, bill pay, and card controls through their iOS app.
description_long:
FORUM Credit Union is a member-owned financial institution that provides deposit and lending products to its members through both physical branches and digital channels. The organization is federally insured by the NCUA (National Credit Union Administration) and operates as an Equal Opportunity Lender, serving its membership base with traditional credit union services. Their primary digital access point is a mobile app available on iOS that enables members to manage their financial lives on-the-go.

FORUM's service offerings include account monitoring with balance and transaction history viewing, mobile check deposits, bill payment functionality, fund transfers, loan and credit card payments, card activation and controls, spending analytics, FICO score access, appointment scheduling, branch and ATM location services, account opening, loan applications, and secure member communication. Members can set up account alerts via push notification, text, or email, and utilize both savings and loan calculators. The app supports biometric security through Touch ID and Face ID authentication, and members can access eStatements and member forms through the platform.

FORUM distinguishes itself as a credit union rather than a traditional bank, meaning it operates on a member-ownership model rather than shareholder profit maximization. Their app includes specific features like merchant name cleanup on transaction displays, location-based card spending summaries, real-time card controls with locking and spending limits, and the ability to designate where cards can be used geographically. The integration of budgeting tools and spend categorization represents an attempt to provide financial wellness features beyond basic banking.

However, user reviews reveal significant technical issues that undermine the platform's usability. Multiple users report scrolling problems on iPad that make reviewing account activity difficult, mobile deposit functionality that has become increasingly glitchy with frozen buttons and unresponsiveness, and budget categorization features that crash the app when users attempt to edit categories. These technical deficiencies suggest the app requires substantial maintenance and updates, creating friction for members who rely on core features for daily financial management. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Account balance and transaction history viewing
Mobile check deposits with pending deposit management
Bill payment and payment history tracking
Account alerts via push notification, text, or email
Card activation, locking, and spending limit management
Merchant spending summaries and location-based analytics
pros: Mobile check deposit feature allows members to deposit checks remotely without visiting a branch
Biometric security with Touch ID and Face ID provides convenient yet secure account access
Real-time card controls including locking, spending limits, and geographic usage restrictions for fraud prevention
Comprehensive spending analytics with merchant name cleanup and location mapping to understand spending patterns
Free access to FICO credit scores for members to monitor their creditworthiness
NCUA federal insurance on deposit products protects member funds up to applicable limits
Integrated budgeting and spending categorization tools to help members track and manage expenses
cons: Significant iPad scrolling issues prevent members from easily reviewing account activity on tablets despite compatibility claims
Mobile deposit functionality has become increasingly unreliable with frozen buttons and unresponsive features requiring app restarts
Budget categorization feature crashes when users attempt to edit or add categories, rendering financial tracking non-functional for affected members
App appears to lack robust QA testing as core features have degraded over time based on user reports from 2020-2024
3.4 out of 5 star rating with multiple complaints about recent technical deterioration suggests ongoing stability issues
best_for: Credit union members seeking basic mobile banking with account monitoring, transfers, and bill payment
Members wanting card controls and real-time fraud alerts with ability to lock cards or set spending limits
Users interested in accessing their FICO score and spending analytics for financial awareness
Members comfortable accepting technical limitations in exchange for credit union member-ownership benefits
diagnosis: FORUM Credit Union is best for existing credit union members who prioritize the member-ownership model and need basic mobile banking functionality, but only if they can tolerate significant technical issues with core features like mobile deposit and budgeting tools. The app's degrading performance over recent years—particularly with scrolling, deposit processing, and category management—makes it unreliable for members who depend heavily on digital banking features.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
